questions.question: Explain the data sparsity problem in N-gram language modeling. Why does it occur and what is its consequence?
expectedPoints: Definition: Most possible N-grams are absent from training data.
Cause: The vocabulary size V leads to V^N possible sequences, which is too large to observe fully.
Consequence: MLE assigns a probability of zero to unseen N-grams, making any sentence containing them impossible.
Solution: Mention smoothing as the general remedy.

questions.question: Compare and contrast Bigram and Trigram language models. Discuss their advantages, disadvantages, and a practical method to combine their strengths.
expectedPoints: Advantage of Trigram: Captures more context, generally more accurate.
Disadvantage of Trigram: Higher data sparsity and larger storage requirements.
Advantage of Bigram: Less sparse, smaller model size.
Disadvantage of Bigram: Less contextual information.
Combination Method: Explain Linear Interpolation, which combines probability estimates from different order models (e.g., trigram, bigram, unigram) using weighting parameters.
